p2.bio: Saramma came to America in 1902 and met Gheevarghese at a church social in Chicago. She was the heart of the family, raising seven children while also assisting as an informal midwife to the neighbourhood. Her soda bread recipe is still made by the family today.

p5.bio: Yohannan was a skilled fisherman who worked the waters of Kerala for most of his life. He moved to Kollam

p7.bio: Baby was mechanically gifted from boyhood and found his calling in Detroit's booming auto industry. He worked as an engineer for Ford for over 30 years, contributing to the design of several iconic post-war models.
p7.notes: Held 3 patents in automotive engineering. Worked at Ford 1935–1967.

p8.bio: Gheevarghese showed artistic talent from a young age. She studied fine art in Chicago and later in Paris, becoming a skilled oil painter and art restorer who worked with several major museums.
p8.notes: Still living. Studied in Paris 1938. Exhibited at the Art Institute of Chicago.

p9.bio: Gheevarghese, the youngest, inherited his father's loud laugh and his mother's warmth.
